title: Kiel Keel and his health problems
display_title: Kiel Keel and his health problems
wikitext:
[]: Kiel (Keel) and his health problems             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 18, 2008 3:55 pm Okay, so I searched and I couldn't find anything (if there is a thread about this topic it could always be merged I guess) but is there ever anything canon or otherwise that explains WHY Kiel is part uh cyborg? And/or why he has that spine and visor of his? I don't remember coming across anything in canon that gave any reasons why, but it's something I've been always curious about. My guess is he got into some type of accident before or shortly after 2nd Impact... Kiel in a paper sometime after 2I Anybody have any thoughts on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Dec 18, 2008 4:24 pm My guess is that they are "improvements" that my have helped to extend his life beyond the normal age. He might have had the luxury of being able to afford the first cyborg implants due to his influence. Or it could just be a symbol for the inhuman nature of Seele's work.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Thu Dec 18, 2008 6:43 pm Because cyborgs are cool and evilll !

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 18, 2008 7:32 pm Holy Diver wrote: My guess is that they are "improvements" that my have helped to extend his life beyond the normal age. He might have had the luxury of being able to afford the first cyborg implants due to his influence.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 18, 2008 8:01 pm AFAIK, there's nothing in canon about why he's a cyborg. Like Sachi and Holy Diver suggested, the implants were probably to prolong his life. He could be in his seventies in that photograph, and he does appear to be in his mid-late eighties in series, if not older. His internal organs are probably all worn out. Notice that we have no indication whatsoever that Keel is a cyborg (That visor doesn't count) until he announces his satisfaction with Third Impact and turns to tang. It suggests that he was cyborgized so that he could live long enough to see 3I/Instrumentality carried out. If he died before the event, he'd probably be left out of instrumentality even if it did succeed. (But that still doesn't rule out an accident entirely).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 18, 2008 9:27 pm That makes a lot of sense. Anybody else feel like throwing their hat into the ring?

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Thu Dec 18, 2008 9:39 pm My guesses have been: 1.) He was diagnosed with some kind of terminal illness (In a fanfic I'm working on, I speculate it was spinal cancer) and wanting to live long enough to see 3I, he had the cyborg parts installed to eliminate the condition. OR 2.) An attempt was made on his life and the only way to keep him alive was to cyborg-ize him.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Dec 18, 2008 10:08 pm this is colored by seeing Ghost in the Shell 3: Solid State Society, but I think its a commentary on the "Graying of Japan", which is worse than it is here: the old are doing whatever they can to prolong their lives at the expense of the young, even unnaturally

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Dec 18, 2008 11:17 pm I agree with Sachi and Holy Diver. Keel wanted to see his dream fufilled.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Dec 19, 2008 1:56 am I think Sachi and Holy Diver got it on the ball. No need to discuss this anymore, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Dec 19, 2008 7:56 am Could be just his age getting the best of him. I think given the fact that we don’t exactly know how old he is this is plausible wikipedia wrote: With unrest in the country, no large-scale search could be undertaken. Sellers attempted to get the Syrians to help him locate the cave, but they demanded more money than he could offer. Finally, Cave 1 was discovered, on January 28, 1949, by a United Nations observer. The Dead Sea Scrolls went up for sale eventually, in an advertisement in the June 1, 1954 Wall Street Journal.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 19, 2008 8:05 am he's not "the Wandering Jew" or something, it's just that he's a greedy old but wealthy and powerful man who uses top-of-the-line cybernetic implants to extend his life as long as possible

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Dec 19, 2008 8:14 am V wrote: he's not "the Wandering Jew"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 21, 2008 2:05 pm I don't really see why he would replace most of his body with all those unwieldy cybernetic parts unless it were required. It's not as though he's decrepitly old in 2001. Injury or wasting/terminal disease have been my favorite "motivators" for him. We talked about Kiel a bit back in the ANF "Adam's embryo..." thread, but it is so batshit insane I'm literally getting dizzy trying to sort out the relevant stuff from the "zOmG hYpOmAnIa iS fUn!!!" stuff. If you are very brave and you want some lulz at my expense, start hunting right around here , where Seele08 shows up. V wrote: I think its a commentary on the "Graying of Japan"

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Dec 21, 2008 3:09 pm Reichu wrote: I don't really see why he would replace most of his body with all those unwieldy cybernetic parts unless it were required. It's not as though he's decrepitly old in 2001. Injury or wasting/terminal disease have been my favorite "motivators" for him.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Dec 21, 2008 3:19 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: You make him sound like Davros or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 21, 2008 4:23 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: You make him sound like Davros or something.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Dec 21, 2008 4:37 pm did Keel furnish his creaking old brain with cybernetic parts, and did the upgrades, if they existed, affect his sense of logic and morality? And, is the visor just more eye candy?

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Sun Dec 21, 2008 5:10 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: did Keel furnish his creaking old brain with cybernetic parts, and did the upgrades, if they existed, affect his sense of logic and morality? And, is the visor just more eye candy?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 21, 2008 6:54 pm ...given that Gainax are a bunch of Clarke fanatics, think that was on purpose?
